classification_rationale: This is a Next.js marketing/brochure website built on the purchased "Axleo" ThemeForest template (package.json name "axleo-next", README:3 "AFI Research Institute website (Next.js), adapted from the Axleo template"). It is the public portal for research programs, services, team, blog, and a fiat-only Stripe "Adopt-an-Agent" donation flow (README:26-33). It contains zero protocol code: no schemas, no contracts, no TSSD/vault/mint/DAG logic, no API routes (find for api dir returned 0), and the README explicitly states it is separate from the AFI Network ("Keep a clear separation from the AFI Network (registry/explorer live elsewhere; we link out when ready)" README:33). The repo carries no normative, reference-implementation, or replay-relevant artifacts and therefore sits outside the portable-protocol surface.
primary_purpose: Public-facing marketing/brochure website (Next.js App Router, Axleo template skin) for the AFI Research Institute, presenting research programs, services, team/careers, blog, and a fiat-only Stripe "Adopt-an-Agent" donation flow; explicitly separate from the AFI Network protocol stack.

notable_findings.title: Repo is the Axleo Next.js marketing template, not protocol code
notable_findings.evidence: package.json:2 "name": "axleo-next"; README.md:3 "AFI Research Institute website (Next.js), adapted from the Axleo template and tailored to our information architecture and the Adopt-an-Agent donation flow."
notable_findings.recommendation: Treat as OUT_OF_SCOPE for the portable-protocol audit; it is a brochure site with no normative or replay artifacts.

notable_findings.title: No protocol patterns present anywhere in source
notable_findings.evidence: grep -rniE 'mongodb|MongoTSSD|TSSD|VaultedSignalRecord|usignal|USS v1|cpj|mintForSignal|coordinateMint|epoch|ReplaySession|determinism|publicSurface|AFIMintCoordinator|froggy|doctrine|BigQuery|kafka|timescaledb|influxdb' over *.js/jsx/ts/tsx/json/md/mjs/sol returned zero matches (excluding lockfile/node_modules).
notable_findings.recommendation: No action; confirms repo holds no schema, contract, vault, DAG, or replay surface.

notable_findings.title: Apparent protocol keywords are marketing copy / template radio labels only
notable_findings.evidence: README.md:40 banner tiles 'Market Observability, Signal Robustness, Governance & Attestation'; README.md:102 'canonical PDFs/notebooks/datasets'; src/utils/ChoosePaymentMethod.js:24 className 'stripe' (UI radio next to PayPal, no backend).
notable_findings.recommendation: None; 'signal'/'canonical' here are research-marketing nouns, and 'stripe'/'supabase' hits are stock template e-commerce UI, not integrations.

notable_findings.title: Site explicitly decoupled from the AFI Network; donations confer no rights and accept no crypto
notable_findings.evidence: README.md:33 'Keep a clear separation from the AFI Network (registry/explorer live elsewhere; we link out when ready).'; README.md:170 'The Institute does not accept crypto/tokens.'; CONTRIBUTING.md:57 'Donations are not purchases and confer no financial rights.'
notable_findings.recommendation: None; correctly avoids collapsing marketing/donation flow into the commitment/evidence planes.

notable_findings.title: Planned data backend is Supabase/Postgres + Stripe + SendGrid, fully off the protocol path
notable_findings.evidence: .env.example 'NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL / SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY / STRIPE_SECRET_KEY / SENDGRID_API_KEY'; README.md:84-86 'Stripe Checkout (donations; webhook -> Supabase) ... Supabase (Postgres) for adopt_requests'.
notable_findings.recommendation: None; this Postgres usage is donation CRM, unrelated to the evidence-plane vault.schema.json multi-engine requirement.

notable_findings.title: Large raw datasets (IRS990, NBER, USASpending, etc.) excluded from git, moved to undocumented external storage
notable_findings.evidence: data/raw/README.md:7 'Large data files ... are excluded from version control'; :13 'Storage location: [To be documented by team]'; :39-40 'Excluded large data files from git to reduce repo size from 1.6GB to <100MB ... Multi-repo reorganization (Phase 1.6)'.
notable_findings.recommendation: Tag for follow-up: document the external storage location so the cited research datasets remain reproducible/auditable, consistent with the site's stated reproducibility claims; not a protocol-surface concern.
